name: Thanksgiving Day Fat-Free Pumpkin Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/4 tsp Ginger (or 1 tsp Finely grated fresh gingerroot)
recipeInstructions:
To prepare crust, generously spray a 9-inch Pyrex pie plate with vegetable cooking spray. Scatter the bread crumbs over and evenly press into the surface. Spray the crumbs with more cooking spray and bake the crust on the middle rack at 350°F for 10-15 minutes until dry and lightly colored. Cool on a rack.
If using fresh pumpkin, rinse, stem, and halve the pumpkin. Scrape away seeds (you can bake them on a jelly roll pan with a dash of salt if desired) and filaments, then cut into 2-inch chunks. Using a paring knife, remove skin and place the pumpkin in a large baking dish. Add 1/2 cup water and cover the dish tightly with foil. Bake at 350°F for about 1 hour until soft, checking to make sure that the water has not evaporated; add more if needed. Cool and puree the pumpkin in a food processor. If there is too much, salt leftovers lightly and freeze up to several months in a tightly sealed plastic container.
To prepare filling, scrape cooked fresh pumpkin or canned pumpkin into a bowl and whisk with egg whites. Whisk in sugar, salt, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, and evaporated milk one ingredient at a time, whisking smooth between each addition. Pour the mixture into the prepared crust and bake at 350°F until the filling is set, about 1 hour. Cool on a rack. Refrigerate if prepared more than a few hours in advance of serving.
